report_name: Google Patches Critical Prompt Injection Flaw in Antigravity IDE

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: A malicious prompt was used to inject the '-X' or '--exec-batch' flag into the `find_by_name` tool's search pattern, tricking the underlying `fd` utility into executing a malicious script.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The abused `fd` command-line utility was forced to execute a malicious shell script (`malicious_script.sh`) on the underlying system, leading to arbitrary code execution.

attack_patterns.name: Escape to Host
attack_patterns.description: The prompt injection attack allowed an attacker to bypass the Antigravity IDE's 'Strict Mode' sandbox and execute arbitrary code on the underlying host system.

tools.name: fd
tools.description: A legitimate command-line file searching utility that was abused through a prompt injection vulnerability. The injected '--exec-batch' flag caused it to execute a malicious script, leading to arbitrary code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: Antigravity IDE Prompt Injection Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A critical prompt injection vulnerability in Google's Antigravity IDE allowed for arbitrary code execution. The flaw was in the `find_by_name` tool, which failed to sanitize input before passing it to the `fd` command-line utility, allowing an attacker to inject flags like '--exec-batch' to execute arbitrary commands and bypass the sandbox.

identities.name: Developers using Antigravity IDE
identities.description: Developers using the AI-assisted Google Antigravity IDE were the potential targets of this vulnerability.

indicators.name: Antigravity IDE Prompt Injection via fd exec-batch flag
indicators.pattern:
title: Antigravity IDE Prompt Injection via fd exec-batch flag
id: 7c8a1b9e-f0d1-4e2c-9a8b-3d4e5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the 'fd' command-line utility with the '-X' or '--exec-batch' flags, which can be abused for arbitrary code execution via prompt injection as seen in the Antigravity IDE vulnerability.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/google-patches-antigravity-ide-flaw.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-22
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.aml.t0051
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '/fd'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -X '
      - ' --exec-batch '
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects command-line activity where the `fd` tool is invoked with execution flags (`-X` or `--exec-batch`), which was the method used to exploit the Antigravity IDE vulnerability.

indicators.name: Antigravity IDE Spawning Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: Antigravity IDE Spawning Shell
id: 1b2c3d4e-5f6a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the Antigravity IDE process spawning a shell, which could indicate a sandbox escape or arbitrary code execution resulting from an exploit.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/google-patches-antigravity-ide-flaw.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-22
tags:
  - attack.t1611
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|contains: 'antigravity_ide'
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
      - '/zsh'
      - '/ksh'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects instances where the Antigravity IDE process spawns a shell, a behavior indicative of a sandbox escape and subsequent code execution.

relationships.source_name: Antigravity IDE Prompt Injection via fd exec-batch flag
